message:
fix(mail-ui/tui-poller): wrap on-demand DB connections in DbConnGuard so drops go through close_sync()

Both `mail_ui::load_recipes` and `tui_poller::refill_missing_detail_lists`
opened a short-lived sync metadata DB connection, ran one batch of
read-only queries against it, and then let it go out of scope. The
underlying `FrankenConnection` emits a `drop_close` WARN
("Connection dropped without explicit close()") on Drop, so every /mail
recipe load and every TUI refill cycle produced the same log line
forever.

Wrap both call sites in `mcp_agent_mail_db::guard_db_conn(conn, tag)` so
the `DbConnGuard`'s Drop impl invokes `close_sync()` before the
`FrankenConnection` sees its own Drop — eliminating the WARN without
changing the query sequence.

The `tag` argument is the call-site identifier used by `DbConnGuard` to
tag any close-path diagnostics:
- `mail_ui::load_recipes`
- `tui_poller::refill_missing_detail_lists`

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
